title: Feature 1: Markdown Rendering in Chat Messages
description: Add markdown rendering for chat messages in both VS Code and JetBrains. Handles fenced code blocks, inline code, bold, italic, links, lists. Foundation for Feature 2 (file paths).
definitionOfDone: VS Code: renderMarkdown() function handles fenced code blocks, inline code, bold, italic, links, lists
VS Code: renderContent() replaces highlightMentions() for message rendering
VS Code: CSS styles for pre, code, strong, em, a, ul/ol/li using VS Code theme variables
JetBrains: JEditorPane replaces JTextArea for message content
JetBrains: markdownToHtml() converts markdown to HTML 3.2 compatible markup
JetBrains: System messages render as italic with system color
npm run compile succeeds in moe-vscode

implementationPlan.description: VS Code: Add `renderMarkdown(text)` function in the webview `
implementationPlan.note: Added renderMarkdown() with fenced code block extraction, inline code, bold, italic, links, unordered/ordered lists, newline-to-br conversion. Falls back to escapeHtml on error. Updated highlightMentions to work on pre-rendered HTML instead of raw text.

implementationPlan.description: VS Code: Replace `highlightMentions()` (line 453) with two functions: `highlightMentions(html)` that applies mention spans to pre-rendered HTML, and `renderContent(text, isSystem)` that returns `<em>` escaped text for system messages, or `highlightMentions(renderMarkdown(text))` for normal messages. Wraps in try/catch, falls back to escapeHtml on error. Update `renderMessage()` line 492 to call `renderContent(msg.content, isSystem)` instead of `highlightMentions(msg.content)`.
implementationPlan.note: Added renderContent(text, isSystem) that returns italic escaped text for system messages, or highlightMentions(renderMarkdown(text)) for normal messages with try/catch fallback. Updated renderMessage() to use renderContent().

implementationPlan.description: VS Code: Update CSS in `.message-content` (lines 228-233). Remove `white-space: pre-wrap`. Add styles for: `pre` (code block background, border, padding, monospace font), `code` (inline code background, small padding), `pre code` (no extra background), `strong`, `em`, `a` (link color from VS Code theme var), `a:hover` (underline), `ul/ol` (left padding 20px), `li` (small margin). All using `var(--vscode-*)` theme variables.
implementationPlan.note: Removed white-space: pre-wrap from .message-content. Added CSS for pre, code, pre code, a, a:hover, ul/ol, li - all using var(--vscode-*) theme variables.

implementationPlan.description:
JetBrains: Add `escapeHtml(text)` and `markdownToHtml(content)` methods to `ChatPanel.kt` before `createMessageBubble()`. escapeHtml replaces &, <, >, ". markdownToHtml: extract fenced code blocks to placeholders, escapeHtml the rest, convert inline code/bold/italic/links/list items to HTML with inline styles (Swing JEditorPane only supports HTML 3.2). Restore code blocks, convert remaining newlines to
. Error handling: safe regex replacements, malformed markdown shows as escaped text.
implementationPlan.note: Added escapeHtml() and markdownToHtml() methods. markdownToHtml uses placeholder extraction for fenced code blocks, then processes inline code, bold, italic, links, unordered/ordered lists. Uses inline styles for HTML 3.2 compatibility with Swing JEditorPane. Falls back to escapeHtml on exception.

implementationPlan.description: JetBrains: Replace `JTextArea` (line 258) with `JEditorPane("text/html", htmlContent)` in `createMessageBubble()`. Compute text color from JBColor, build HTML body using `markdownToHtml()` for normal messages or `escapeHtml()` for system messages. Set `isEditable=false`, `isOpaque=false`, `HONOR_DISPLAY_PROPERTIES=true`. Add imports: `javax.swing.JEditorPane`, `javax.swing.UIManager`. Error handling: wrap markdownToHtml in try/catch, fall back to escapeHtml.
implementationPlan.note: Replaced JTextArea with JEditorPane("text/html", ...). System messages render as italic escaped text. Normal messages use markdownToHtml() with try/catch fallback to escapeHtml. Added JEditorPane import. Set HONOR_DISPLAY_PROPERTIES for proper font rendering.

implementationPlan.description: Test and verify: Run `cd moe-vscode && npm run compile` (VS Code compiles). Run `cd moe-jetbrains && ./gradlew buildPlugin` (JetBrains compiles). Manual test: send markdown message in chat, verify rendered formatting.
implementationPlan.note: VS Code compiles cleanly. JetBrains BUILD SUCCESSFUL. Removed unused JTextArea import.

comments.author: architect
comments.content: PRIORITY: User's primary IDE is PyCharm/JetBrains. Implement JetBrains markdown rendering (JEditorPane + markdownToHtml) FIRST, then VS Code. Test in PyCharm before VS Code.
